approvedIndications: Reduction of excess abdominal fat in HIV-infected patients with lipodystrophy
offLabelNotes: Prescribed off-label for visceral fat reduction in non-HIV populations; evidence is limited and supervision is advised.

mechanism.mechanismSummary: Tesamorelin is a synthetic analog of growth hormone-releasing hormone (GHRH) that binds GHRH receptors on somatotrophs, stimulating pulsatile endogenous GH release and downstream IGF-1 elevation.

reconstitution.instructions: Swirl gently until dissolved; do not shake. Inspect for particulates before injection.

indications.population: HIV-infected adults with lipodystrophy and excess abdominal fat
indications.benefitSummary: Reduces visceral adipose tissue and improves body composition over 26 weeks of therapy.

dose.scheduleText: 1.4 mg SC once daily, preferably in the abdomen, rotating injection sites.

notes: Label-directed dose. Alternate injection site abdominal quadrants to reduce local reactions.

compatibility.vialCompatibilitySummary: Reconstitute with the supplied sterile water only. Do not co-mix with other peptides in the same vial prior to administration.

blendingRules.message: Do not reconstitute or co-mix tesamorelin with other peptides; label specifies sterile water for injection only.

contraindications.item: Disruption of hypothalamic-pituitary axis (hypophysectomy, hypopituitarism, pituitary tumor/surgery, head irradiation, head trauma)

contraindications.notes: Discontinue therapy if active malignancy is diagnosed.

warnings.notes: Monitor IGF-1 periodically; consider dose interruption if persistently elevated >3x ULN.
Assess glucose tolerance prior to initiation and periodically during therapy.
May cause peripheral edema and arthralgia.

precautions.notes: Increased mortality reported with GH therapy in acutely critically ill patients; consider discontinuation.

interactions.mechanism: Chronic supraphysiologic corticosteroid exposure can blunt GH axis response.
interactions.recommendedAction: Use lowest effective corticosteroid dose; reassess tesamorelin response.

interactions.mechanism: GH elevation may alter CYP3A4 activity.
interactions.effect: Potential altered plasma concentrations of simvastatin, cyclosporine.

avoidWith.target: Other GH secretagogues (CJC-1295, Sermorelin, Ipamorelin) used concurrently
avoidWith.reason: Additive GH/IGF-1 elevation with no labeled support; increased adverse-event risk.

overlapRules.message: Combining with other GH axis agents duplicates pathway activation.
overlapRules.action: Flag for review; do not auto-approve.

redundancyRules.action: Consolidate to a single GHRH analog.

timingRules.message: Evening dosing aligns with nocturnal GH pulse.

nutrition.guidance: Adequate dietary protein (1.2-1.6 g/kg/day) supports lean mass retention during visceral fat reduction.

sleep.guidance: Prioritize 7-9 hours of sleep; endogenous GH pulses peak during slow-wave sleep and complement tesamorelin therapy.

exercise.guidance: Combine with resistance training 2-3x/week to preserve lean mass during fat loss.

applicabilityNotes: Guidance is enrichment-only and does not alter label-directed dosing.

claimSpecificEvidence.claim: Reduces visceral adipose tissue in HIV-lipodystrophy
Improves body composition in non-HIV populations

evidenceGaps: Long-term (>52 week) safety data in non-HIV populations.
controversies: Off-label use for general body composition lacks strong RCT support.

sourceRecords.title: EGRIFTA SV (tesamorelin for injection) Prescribing Information
Effects of Tesamorelin on Visceral Fat and Liver Fat in HIV-Infected Patients

curationNotes: ClassA source (FDA label) establishes regulatory, dosing, safety, and monitoring canon.
